info.name: Too Many Roads
info.attribution:
Layout, board, cards, design, and coding by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and released to the Public Domain under CC0.

The library image by LawDawg96 is released to the Public Domain under CC0.
info.description: Build a road network while managing limited resources. Try to get the most valuable settlements for yourself and block your opponents.
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press the Setup button to begin. A random player (indicated by a button next to the seat area) is chosen to start. The point values of the settlements is also randomized. To complete setup, take turns placing the hex workshop tile on a light green plain area. For a 2-player game, alternate turns placing two workshop tiles.

Drag and drop road tiles and settlements according to the rules. Click a road tile to rotate it. Click the "Click to end turn" button between all turns. Resource points and victory points are automatically calculated. In the event you need to change the value of a player's resource or victory points, click there and enter the number gained or lost.

fields.label: Check this box and click the green button to reset the game.
Choose cursor style for all players.

thenRoutine.note: this branch of the IF is called directly after you start dragging the card

elseRoutine.note: this branch of the IF is called when the card exits the holder entirely

fields.label: Type the number of resources points or lost. Put a - before the number to lose points.
